curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: I got so hammered at the Grammy afterparty in '68 that I argued with Aretha for forty-five minutes about whether chili dogs counted as a vegetable. I still think I made some valid points. She did not agree.
quotes.speaker: Janis Joplin
quotes.context: Oral history interview conducted by Rolling Stone contributing editor David Dalton, San Francisco, June 1970, published posthumously in the 1971 retrospective piece 'Full Tilt: The Last Year of Janis'
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Janis's rowdy afterparty reputation and her warm, specific storytelling style make this feel real, and the Aretha name-drop grounds it in her actual world. The giveaway is the comedic punchline structure; Janis's real anecdotes tended to trail off rather than land cleanly.

quotes.text: Over the past forty years I've been loaded on booze, coke, acid, Quaaludes, glue, cough mixture, heroin, Rohypnol, Klonopin, Vicodin, and too many other heavy-duty substances to list in this footnote. On more than a few occasions I was on all of these at the same time.
quotes.speaker: Ozzy Osbourne
quotes.context: From a footnote in his 2010 autobiography 'I Am Ozzy' (with Chris Ayres), cataloguing four decades of his own substance abuse.

quotes.text: I threw up on a promoter's shoes in Düsseldorf in '72 and the son of a bitch still booked us for the spring tour. That's either loyalty or he didn't look down. Either way, I respect the man.
quotes.speaker: John Bonham
quotes.context: Backstage interview with NME journalist Nick Kent at the Earl's Court Arena, London, May 1975
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Bonham's infamous road-chaos reputation makes this completely plausible, and Kent did conduct real NME access pieces around that era. The giveaway is 'I respect the man' — Bonham's actual recorded speech was blunter and less wryly appreciative.

quotes.text: I think weed is the best drug on earth. One time I smoked a joint with peyote in it, and I saw a wolf howling at the moon. Hollywood is a coke town, but weed is so much better. And molly, too. Those are happy drugs – social drugs. They make you want to be with friends. You're out in the open. You're not in a bathroom. I really don't like coke. It's so gross and so dark. It's like what are you, from the Nineties? Ew.
quotes.speaker: Miley Cyrus
quotes.context: Miley Cyrus ranking her drug preferences in her September 2013 Rolling Stone cover story, calling weed and molly 'happy drugs' and dismissing cocaine as dated.

source.title: Miley Cyrus: 'Weed Is the Best Drug on Earth' (Rolling Stone)

quotes.text: A couple nights ago, I was licking jelly off my boyfriend's penis. And I thought, "Oh my God — I'm turning into my mother!"
quotes.speaker: Sarah Silverman
quotes.context: From her 2005 stand-up special "Sarah Silverman: Jesus Is Magic." Her signature sweet-faced delivery lands a graphic sex image as the setup, with the punchline being the realization she's inheriting her mother's habits.

quotes.text: Marriage is a wonderful institution. But who wants to live in an institution? I've said that. Everybody's said that. But I mean it in a dirtier way than most people do.
quotes.speaker: Groucho Marx
quotes.context: Appearance on The Dick Cavett Show, ABC Studios, New York, September 1969
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the first two sentences are a well-known paraphrase floating around Groucho's era (often misattributed to him anyway), so grounding it with 'I've said that, everybody's said that' sounds exactly like his self-aware meta-humor. The giveaway is the last clause; Groucho's real innuendo was typically more elliptical and never announced itself as dirty.
